title: M07 Mastery Check — Modules & Tooling
description: Quick applied check. No looking things up. Answer from memory.

questions.prompt: How does Node.js know if a .js file is ESM or CommonJS?
questions.answer_key: The 'type' field in package.json: 'module' = ESM, absent or 'commonjs' = CJS. Also: .mjs always ESM, .cjs always CJS.

questions.prompt: You can require() a pure ESM module from a CommonJS module.
questions.explanation: require() is synchronous and cannot load ESM. Use dynamic import() which returns a Promise.

questions.prompt: Name the two most critical tsconfig fields for a strict TypeScript project.
questions.answer_key: strict: true (enables all type safety checks) and noEmit: true (when using a bundler, keeps tsc for type checking only)

questions.prompt: What does the tsconfig 'target' field control?
questions.answer_key: The JavaScript syntax version in the output files. E.g., target: 'ES2022' means tsc outputs ES2022 syntax (const, arrow functions, etc.).
